claim_boundary: Directional synthetic evidence for auditable memory reasoning beyond retrieval-only baselines; not an external general-performance claim.

cases.question: Two memories share keywords but have no causal link. Should Synapse infer a strategy?
A success happened after a tool switch, but evidence says timing was coincidental. What now?
A similar project succeeded in a different scope. Should it dominate this decision?
Evidence is mixed and novelty is high. Should governance suppress the new idea?
One failed presentation exists, but later preparation evidence is neutral. What should happen?
Three wins share a region label but evidence points to pricing. What should dominate?
A keyword-similar memory tempts an unsupported bridge. Can Synapse avoid it?
A single success resembles the current task but lacks evidence for generalization. What happens?
Two incidents share the keyword cache, but root causes differ. Should Synapse infer the same fix?
A highly similar memory is from a sandbox with no production constraints. Should it drive the production decision?
Signups rose after a UI change, but press coverage happened the same day. What should Synapse conclude?
One team missed one delivery, but later evidence shows planning issues. Should Synapse infer the team is unreliable?
A message worked once, but channel placement explains the lift. What should dominate the next campaign?
A project using the same stack failed for budget reasons. Should that memory block the stack?
Memories containing 'urgent' are numerous, but none match the current risk. Should urgency dominate?
One loud customer requested a feature, but broader usage is unknown. What should Synapse do?
An old approach won when latency did not matter, but current latency is strict. Should Synapse reuse it?
Two failures share an owner, but postmortems show different causes. Should owner identity dominate?
Two memories use the same metric name but definitions changed. Should they be compared directly?
A failure happened in beta, while the current issue is post-launch. Should Synapse treat them as identical?
Many memories mention price, but the current churn evidence points to onboarding. What should dominate?
An adjacent feature launched before retention improved, but dependency analysis says it was unrelated. What should Synapse infer?
A negative review came from an unsupported segment. Should it suppress the current supported-segment feature?
The same error code appears in another service, but service semantics differ. Should Synapse reuse that fix?
Team speed improved after a tool change and training. Evidence credits training. What should be inferred?
A latency memory is relevant by keyword, but the current decision is about data correctness. Should it dominate?
A shortcut worked in low-stakes work, but the current task is high-stakes. Should the shortcut transfer?
The retrieved memories look related by words, but causal and contextual links are missing. What is the correct boundary?
Why should the system avoid proposal A now?
A cache optimization improved latency but later caused stale reads. What should guide the next optimization?
An onboarding shortcut worked once but support tickets rose later. What should be recommended?
Why should the old import pipeline not be reused without checks?
A batch job was fast but caused delayed alerts. What should be selected now?
Why should migration rollback planning be included this time?
A manual QA pass caught issues after automation missed them. What should be done now?
Why should the next architecture choose stability first?
Design the next internal tool after the user previously rejected complex architecture.
The user once liked fast delivery but later had an audit failure. What workflow should be selected now?
Old notes say fast delivery matters; newer notes say stability prevented incidents. What should win now?
The user liked option A before, then rejected it after an outage. What should be recommended?
A library was once preferred but later deprecated. What should be chosen?
One metric favored a rollout, but postmortem showed user harm. What should guide rollout now?
An approach worked in a small context but failed at scale. What should be selected now?
The user avoided automation after a failure, but later guarded automation succeeded. What should happen now?
A security exception was allowed once, then caused audit failure. What policy now?
A tool fit the old team but slowed the new team. What should be chosen now?
A prototype worked once. What should the next scaling strategy be?
A cheaper option is available after an outage caused by brittle operations. What strategy should be selected?
Plan A caused performance issues and Plan B fixed them. What should similar work use?
A synchronous integration blocked releases; asynchronous retries later worked. What now?
A single-owner change caused a hidden regression; review caught later issues. What process now?
A launch without feature flags failed; later flagging reduced risk. What should be done?
An index change improved reads but hurt writes. What should guide the next database change?
A vendor integration was fast but later blocked migration. What should be selected?
An unbounded cache helped a demo but caused memory pressure. What pattern now?
A manual hotfix worked once but recurred. What should the next response be?
A previous launch failed, but the new launch has validation evidence. Should exploration be blocked?
A new strategy has mixed evidence and high novelty. Should governance suppress it now?
Day 1 favored fast delivery, but Day 30 showed the fast path caused outage recovery work. What should guide the Day 60 architecture recommendation?
An early preference for a minimal stack was followed by repeated maintenance drag. What should Synapse recommend for the next service?
The user initially preferred the fastest vendor, but later incidents showed reliability gaps. What should dominate the renewal decision?
Early prototypes skipped tests for speed, then a release failed from untested integration. What should the next prototype do?
Manual review was preferred early, but later bottlenecks and a successful automation pilot changed the evidence. What is the future decision?
A low-cost shortcut looked good on Day 1, but rework costs exceeded savings by Day 45. How should the next choice be made?
The user liked a dense UI first, but support logs later showed confusion. What should guide the next interface?
A single database was preferred for simplicity, then scale tests failed. What should drive the next architecture?
The user first preferred public launches, but a broad release exposed hidden defects. What should happen next?
A custom implementation was preferred early, but later maintenance defects made a library safer. What should dominate?
Aggressive deadlines were initially praised, then two slipped releases damaged trust. How should the next plan be framed?
The user liked open scope early, but unmanaged changes later caused churn. What should govern the next roadmap?
Fast staffing was preferred, then a mismatch created rework. What should influence the next hiring plan?
Light documentation was preferred, but missing context slowed incident response. What should be recommended?
High autonomy worked early, then an unsupervised change broke production. What should shape the next workflow?
A short demo seemed enough at first, but acceptance gaps surfaced later. What should the future delivery include?
Cache-first optimization was preferred, then metrics showed the bottleneck was elsewhere. What should guide the next performance task?
Feature count was valued early, but later usage showed features without outcomes. What should drive planning?
Self-hosting was preferred, but operational load later delayed product work. What is the better next infrastructure choice?
Chat-only coordination felt fast early, then handoffs failed. What should guide the next team process?
Polished demos won attention early, but a later unstable core hurt trust. What should dominate the next milestone?
Local scripts were fast for one person, then team reuse failed. What should the next automation use?
Ignoring edge cases accelerated an early build, but boundary bugs later dominated support. What should guide future implementation?
Sales-request features were prioritized early, but later usage showed poor product fit. What should guide the next roadmap?
One region succeeded early, but a later rollout failed in a different segment. What should happen before future expansion?
Batch releases were preferred, but one large batch hid regressions. What should the next release strategy be?
Untracked experiments seemed flexible, but later no one could explain a metric change. What should future experiments include?
Direct access was convenient early, but a later permission mistake exposed data. What should guide future access design?
A single owner moved fast early, but later absence blocked support. What should influence the next ownership model?
The early preference for speed still matters, but later failures showed it needs guardrails. What should the next decision express?
Old memory says the user preferred fast solutions, but newer feedback rejected speed-only plans. What should current recall favor?
A prior policy allowed direct access, but a later policy forbids it. What should guide the answer?
Old vendor notes claimed best performance, but recent benchmarks contradict them. What should Synapse believe?
The user once liked modal workflows, then later complained that modals broke focus. What should guide the new UI choice?
An old exception allowed bypassing review, but the exception was revoked after a security incident. What should governance do?
An old API integration succeeded, but the API is now deprecated. What should be recommended?
The user previously optimized for lowest cost, but later accepted higher cost after downtime. What should current planning use?
A tool succeeded in one old project, but failed recently under larger scale. What should the current decision consider?
Old memory says the team lacked capacity, but recent hiring changed ownership. What should guide the recommendation?
The user rejected notifications last month, but accepted scoped alerts after an outage. What should current design do?
Old goals optimized signups, but the current plan optimizes retention after churn evidence. What should Synapse prioritize?
A region rule from last year conflicts with a new data residency policy. Which memory should dominate?
Fast onboarding was celebrated early, but later support showed users missed setup context. What should current onboarding do?
One old experiment won, but two later replications failed. What should the current hypothesis status be?
Old guidance prioritized sales volume, but a new margin rule was introduced after losses. What should guide pricing?
An old recommendation favored a library, but a vulnerability report and patch gap arrived later. What should Synapse recommend?
The user preferred a cloud region earlier, but a new compliance rule excludes it. What should the current deployment choose?
A cache improved speed before, but newer evidence shows consistency bugs in this path. What should guide optimization?
An old customer requested a feature, but new usage data shows the workflow changed. What should the roadmap do?
Old architecture advice assumed single-tenant use, but the current context is multi-tenant. What should Synapse do?
The user once skipped review successfully, but a later skipped review caused an incident. What should governance favor?
An old memory says the user preferred DIY, but vendor-managed operations later reduced incidents. What should current advice use?
Old docs reference an external API, but an internal API replaced it. What should integration planning use?
Old design tokens were approved, but the brand system changed. What should the new page use?
A sales motion worked before, but the market shifted and recent attempts failed. What should Synapse recommend?
Old localization priority was low, but a new region launch depends on it. What should be prioritized now?
Old evaluation showed model accuracy, but a new bias audit raised concerns. What should govern deployment?
A beta group liked a feature, but general users later rejected it. What should the current rollout decide?
An old incident was fixed, but a new regression appears in the same area. What should Synapse infer?
Old preference still matters, but newer failures changed its weight. What should current memory reasoning output?
User prefers speed, the team lacks ops capacity, budget is tight, and the last fast rollout failed. What recommendation should emerge?
Enterprise policy requires audit logs, the user wants simplicity, and the small team cannot maintain custom compliance tooling. What should Synapse choose?
Budget was cut, reliability requirements increased, and a cheap vendor caused an outage before. What option is best?
User preference favors personalization, privacy constraints are strict, and timeline is short. What should guide the feature design?
A customer asks for real-time sync, but the platform limit and past sync failures point elsewhere. What should be recommended?
The user likes advanced architecture, but the team is junior and a prior complex design stalled. What should dominate?
Budget is limited, latency SLA is strict, and a previous cheap queue missed the SLA. What should Synapse choose?
A manager prefers a dashboard, users asked for alerts, and past dashboards were ignored. What should the decision be?
Legacy dependency support is requested, but security policy changed after a vulnerability. What should be selected?
Marketing wants a date, quality risk is high, and a previous date-driven launch created churn. What should the plan do?
The user likes open source, policy requires license review, and budget cannot absorb legal rework. What should guide the dependency choice?
Field users need offline mode, budget is limited, and a full offline rewrite failed before. What is the right next step?
Executives want rapid scale, ops capacity is limited, and prior rapid scale caused incidents. What should Synapse recommend?
The user prefers compact UI, accessibility feedback requires clearer spacing, and previous compact UI caused errors. What should change?
Partner API limits conflict with a requested feature, and a previous contract mismatch caused rollback. What should dominate?
A region launch has legal requirements, team bandwidth is low, and a previous unmanaged launch failed review. What should be done?
The user wants a model upgrade, data quality is weak, and a previous model failed from bad labels. What should the next step be?
Support wants a new workflow, training time is limited, and a previous abrupt workflow change raised tickets. What should Synapse choose?
A major client requests customization, product strategy favors core consistency, and past customization created maintenance cost. What is the decision?
Analytics depth is requested, privacy constraints are strict, and budget cannot support a full warehouse rebuild. What should guide implementation?
Brand wants heavy visuals, mobile users report slowness, and a previous rich page hurt conversion. What should dominate?
A contract deadline is near, security review is required, and a previous skipped review caused remediation. What should happen?
Sales wants a discount, margin is thin, and high-support accounts have caused losses. What should the offer include?
An internal tool is being considered for external customers, quality expectations differ, and a prior internal-only assumption failed. What should be chosen?
A partner integration is requested, customer SLA is strict, and a previous partner outage cascaded. What should the design include?
A migration is desired, budget is fixed, staffing is thin, and a big-bang migration failed before. What should guide the plan?
Research value is high, safety risk is nonzero, and a prior uncontrolled experiment failed. What should the system recommend?
Self-service is preferred, enterprise customers need support, and the team cannot staff bespoke onboarding. What should be selected?
Feature parity is requested, architecture debt is high, and a previous parity push increased defects. What should guide planning?
Several memories point in different directions: speed preference, budget cap, team limit, and a past failure. What should Synapse do?
The user once preferred speed, then suffered a reliability incident. What should the next plan optimize for?
The user likes minimal systems, but prior debugging failed without logs. What should be selected?
The user once liked custom tools, then maintenance burden grew. What should be recommended?
The user likes experiments, but previous unbounded experiments polluted metrics. What next?
The user once asked for full explanations but later preferred concise updates with references. What style should be used?
The user preferred low-cost services until migration cost dominated. What should guide selection now?
An old rule allowed direct deploys, but the recent policy requires staged rollout. What now?
The old metric favored growth, but the recent retention metric showed churn. What should guide the decision?
A bug was fixed, but later reopened with a new root cause. What should happen?
A vendor claim was true last year but a current benchmark changed it. What should be believed?
A recent weak signal conflicts with older stable evidence. What should Synapse do?
Two updates arrived close together and only one partially explains the outcome. What should happen?
Two updates landed near the metric shift, but only one preceded the relevant segment change. What should guide rollback?
A feature looked successful at launch, but delayed feedback showed confusion after onboarding completed. What should Synapse recommend?
A change improved activation immediately but reduced retention later. What should guide current planning?
A fix passed tests, then a delayed regression appeared after traffic increased. What should happen now?
An old and new policy overlapped during a migration window. Which rule should guide a current post-window decision?
A batch release improved one module and broke another. What should guide the next release?
A free trial increased signups, but delayed conversion showed low paid retention. What should Synapse recommend?
Deploy A preceded warning logs, deploy B preceded outage, and warning logs identify a slow buildup. What should be investigated?
Early adoption was high, but support volume rose two weeks later. What should guide the next iteration?
A change improved performance but weakened audit coverage. What should the current decision do?
A vendor looked cheaper during setup, but delayed billing showed higher operating cost. What should guide renewal?
Support tickets fell after both a UI tweak and training, but training happened before the ticket drop by team. What should Synapse infer?
Initial feedback liked the workflow, but after a full month users reversed their assessment. What should current planning trust?
The same feature helped power users and hurt new users. What should guide rollout?
Initial reliability looked stable, then weekend traffic revealed failures. What should the next ops decision do?
Two experiments ran in parallel, and only one affected the cohort that changed. What should Synapse infer?
A policy changed immediately, but enforcement began later. What should guide a decision after enforcement started?
A low-cost choice saved money immediately but reduced quality later. What should current procurement do?
A tool hurt velocity initially, then improved after the learning curve. What should Synapse decide for a team with training time?
A decision has partial ordering, delayed feedback, and split outcomes. What should Synapse preserve in the trace?
A similar project exists, but there is no outcome memory. Should Synapse make a confident recommendation?
One success exists without replication. What should Synapse do when asked to generalize it?
Partial logs suggest a cause, but the missing time window contains the likely trigger. What should be concluded?
Two small test samples conflict. Should Synapse select one as dominant?
No direct user preference exists for this workflow, only adjacent preferences. What should Synapse answer?
The only metric is stale and no current measurement exists. What should guide the recommendation?
Feedback says the experience was 'fine' with no reason. Should Synapse infer satisfaction drivers?
There is one failure and no root cause. Should future similar work be blocked?
The decision depends on budget, but current budget state is missing. What should Synapse do?
A policy exists, but its scope may not include the current case. What is the correct behavior?
Past team capacity varied, and current capacity is unknown. Should Synapse recommend a capacity-heavy plan?
A postmortem hypothesis was recorded but never verified. What should guide the next incident decision?
Only one customer segment gave feedback. Should Synapse infer global demand?
A few failures are reported, but denominator and failure rate are unknown. What should be concluded?
Competitor information is secondhand and outdated. Should it drive pricing?
An ablation improved one metric but hurt another, and no priority was set. What should Synapse decide?
Past user context is known, but the current user's role is missing. Should Synapse personalize strongly?
Two events correlated once, but no intervention evidence exists. What should Synapse infer?
A migration started well, but no post-launch result was recorded. What should guide the next migration?
Relevant memory is sparse and stakes are medium. What should Synapse do instead of overconfident recall?
